---
title: GSA SAM.gov Business Identity Attribute Service — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: gsa-sam-attribute-service
adapter-class: conformance
mission-class: identity
status: reserved
phase: phase-planning
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-05-07'
---

# GSA SAM.gov Business Identity Attribute Service — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `reserved` · **Class:** `conformance` · **Mission:** `identity` · **Phase:** `phase-planning`

**Canon source:** `canon/adapter-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The GSA SAM.gov Business Identity Attribute Service Adapter is the **federal-business-identity attribute observer** of the UIAO substrate. As a conformance-class identity adapter, it observes business-identity disclosures from GSA SAM.gov — the federal authority of record for the Unique Entity Identifier (UEI) and business registration data used in federal contracting and grants — and emits canonical claims with the disclosure-audit envelope.

SAM.gov serves a public-data API; nevertheless, the reciprocal-consumption entitlement model still applies for audit-grade disclosures. The entitlement record ties each disclosure to the consuming engagement (federal contract, federal grant award, exclusion screening) so the evidence trail remains intact.

Three primary functions:

1. **UEI + business registration observation.** Captures UEI assignments, business legal name, DBA names, address, NAICS codes, registration status (Active / Inactive), and expiration date from SAM.gov, normalizing into canonical claim shape per entity.
2. **Exclusion-list observation.** Watches SAM.gov exclusion-list entries (suspensions, debarments, other exclusion actions) and emits canonical event claims that downstream consumers (contract-eligibility decisions, payment release gates) route through their own evidence streams.
3. **Federal contract-eligibility correlation.** For consuming engagements that perform contract-eligibility decisions, correlates the SAM.gov registration status and exclusion-list state with the canonical decision audit so the downstream decision is traceable to the underlying SAM.gov data.

**Implementation:** Reserved slot per ADR-049. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `uei-verification`, `business-registration`, `exclusion-list`, `federal-contract-eligibility`

**Reads:** SAM.gov public-data API for entity records, registration status, NAICS code assignments, and exclusion-list entries. Public-data access; entitlement still recorded in `canon/reciprocal-consumption-registry.yaml` for audit-grade disclosures.

**Emits:** `sam-attribute-response.json` (per-disclosure canonical claim with UEI, legal name, status, NAICS, exclusion state), `sam-disclosure-audit.json` (per-disclosure audit record with entitlement reference and authority citation), plus `EvidenceObject` records that feed the IA-2 / IA-4 / CA-9 control evidence stream.

**Does NOT:** issue or modify UEI assignments (GSA is the authority of record), modify SAM.gov registrations, add or remove exclusion-list entries, or perform contract-award decisions (decisions are downstream consumer responsibility; the adapter is observation only).

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `IA-2`, `IA-4`, `AU-2`, `AU-12`, `CA-9`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **IA-2** Identification and Authentication | Supporting | UEI claims supplement identification when an entity-anchored attribute is required by a downstream contracting decision. |
| **IA-4** Identifier Management | Primary | UEI is the canonical federal entity identifier; the adapter records UEI as the stable id per entity claim. |
| **AU-2** Event Logging | Primary | Every disclosure is logged with the canonical disclosure-audit envelope and entitlement reference. |
| **AU-12** Audit Record Generation | Supporting | Audit records carry the canonical provenance envelope per ADR-006. |
| **CA-9** Internal System Connections | Supporting | Federal-contract-eligibility decisions are downstream consumers; the adapter provides the canonical authority data for those decisions. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `TBD` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `TBD` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

GSA SAM.gov is the federal authority of record for the Unique Entity Identifier (UEI) and business registration data used in federal contracting and grants. Public-data API; the reciprocal-consumption entitlement model still applies for audit-grade disclosures. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

Member of the federal attribute-service family — pairs with [`treasury-ofac-attribute-service`](../treasury-ofac-attribute-service/treasury-ofac-attribute-service.html) (sanctions-screening complement for federal-contracting eligibility) and [`irs-tin-attribute-service`](../irs-tin-attribute-service/irs-tin-attribute-service.html) (TIN-verification complement).

ZTMM pillars: identity, applications-and-workloads.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-049 — Reserved-slot allocation framework

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
